entities.name: 智能体循环 (agent loop)
entities.how_described: 本集反复强调它是当前真正稀缺的技能：深刻理解 AI 接收指令、执行、反馈并不断迭代的过程。Max 认为理解它的唯一途径就是用构成它的材料（代码）去亲手构建它们。

evidence.quote: If they know how to tweak small details of the UI, but they don't understand how an agent loop works, I would much rather take the designer or PM that deeply has an affinity for understanding how agent loops work and can design those than someone who can write traditional software and tweak styles.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

entities.name: 可塑软件 (malleable software)
entities.how_described: 本集将其定义为更符合使用者利益，而非更符合开发公司利益的软件。Max 指出当前 App 每一层都被死死粘合，就像一个连客厅都不能重新布置的家，而 AI 将让人们重新获得对计算生活的所有权。

evidence.quote: And as people start to ... as you talk about malleable software, we'll come back to this, but like malleable roles almost, there's something we lose like clear career paths and design consistency, things like that.

evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: Malleable software is the idea that software works closer to the interest of the people that use it than the interest of the corporation that makes it, maybe that's how I'd frame it.

evidence.quote: Now, you brought this up presumably because I think you may have not thought about malleable software too much before AI, but now you're making your own tools maybe for podcast recording, for prepping for shows or, I don't know, whatever.

evidence.quote: And that is a form of malleable software, but it has to be built on top of a platform or an operating system that encourages this because otherwise, we're just doing individual ...

evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

entities.name: 品味 (taste)
entities.how_described: 本集将其定义为一种神经模拟：在脑海中运行一台虚拟机，给定一个想法，预测特定群体是否会喜欢它。Max 认为品味的建立离不开大量带反馈的迭代，就像训练模型一样，这也是 AI 时代的核心工作。

evidence.quote: Taste comes up a lot now.

evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

entities.name: 行动力 (agency)

entities.name: 代码 (code)
entities.how_described: 本集极力主张产品经理和设计师应当用代码进行思考和原型设计，因为用代码思考能迫使人直面并理解正在设计的「材料」，而不是仅仅停留在 Figma 静态图中。

evidence.quote: I've always been camp designers should code.

entities.name: 推理 (inference)

entities.name: Claude Code
entities.how_described: 本集提到它是一款让产品和设计人员能直接在终端中编写代码并进行迭代的 AI 编码工具。

evidence.quote: So, for example, I think the two extremes would be if a PM or a designer knows how to tweak with, pick your favorite, they're all the same, Codex, Claude Code or whatever.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: One of the funniest things that I see again and again, I just had the head of product for Claude Code on the podcast, Cat Wu, and she talks about how Slack is basically the OS for Anthropic.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: I think the terminal is actually surprising, which is it's initially scary for people and you could do so much, but now PMs are slowly ... Once they're in Claude Code or Codex, everything is fine.

evidence.quote: Versus if you think about what Claude Code and Codex does is you're coding and then you publish the work of you plus Claude and you get bragging rights of how good of a developer you are.

entities.name: Figma
entities.how_described: 本集指出 Notion 的部分设计师对 Figma 的使用量正在下降，转而直接在代码中建立原型。Max 认为 Figma 的核心超能力是实时协作与非实时协作的无缝融合。

evidence.quote: I think that would have probably happened anyways, but I can tell you the origin story of it, which is when I joined Notion, we were building a lot of chat interfaces and we were designing the chat interfaces in Figma.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: I feel so uncomfortable predicting the future in terms of where things are heading because, well, predicting exponentials is really hard, but I'll take the stab at, it is very, very useful for designers to move from manipulating Figma documents into code.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: One is we have designers who now mostly code and prototype in code, and then they are asked by other teams in marketing and so on to reverse engineer that in Figma because they use that to create assets for videos and so on.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: And that led to first spending more time in Figma instead of writing long PRDs.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: And now it's just, why do I have to do the Figma thing?
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: And so what is the future of Figma, for example, is a really interesting example to me because on one hand, I do see a drop in usage of Figma in some designers at Notion.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: You mentioned Figma is trending down within the design org, which is really interesting.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: So I'm actually not positive that Figma is trending down.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: I could totally believe the Jevons paradox, which is Figma is actually going up.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: So I don't want to perpetuate that with the Figma versus coding.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: Figma, it's the seamless blend between real-time collaboration and not.

entities.name: Heroku
entities.how_described: 本集提到它当年的核心超能力是单行部署命令 `Git Push Heroku master`，这让部署网页应用变得极度简单。

evidence.quote: He ran the design team at Heroku.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: In the past life, I worked at Heroku and Kubernetes was much more successful than Heroku, even though I think from a user experience perspective, it was much worse.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: But the delta was Heroku was saying, "Hey, we're going to replace your ops team."
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: At Heroku, for sure, I think it was the Git Push Heroku master of ... at the time it was really hard to deploy apps.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: It's sad because people don't remember Heroku.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: Git Push Heroku master was just this very simple one-liner that went from the thing on my computer, now I have a URL, and that's so intoxicating that everything else flows from there.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

entities.name: Anthropic
entities.how_described: 本集将其作为优秀模型公司的代表提及，其内部极度依赖 Slack 运作，且认为其虽起步晚但现在占据主导地位。

evidence.quote: What do OpenAI, Anthropic, Cursor, Vercel, Replit, Sierra, Clay, and hundreds of other winning companies all have in common?
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: One of the funniest things that I see again and again, I just had the head of product for Claude Code on the podcast, Cat Wu, and she talks about how Slack is basically the OS for Anthropic.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: No, people at Anthropic, their time is better spent on building AGI than trying to build better Slack.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: If I look at the really, really fast models like Spark, and I forget what the Anthropic variant is where-
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: I would assume they pale in comparison to the folks at OpenAI and Anthropic just by the nature of the work they do and so on.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: In general, I really, really dislike the rivalry discourse that exists in Silicon Valley, which is for Anthropic to win, OpenAI needs to lose and vice versa and that kind of thing.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: I think speaking of models, a good example is Anthropic, which was way behind, started after OpenAI, got less funding and now is just killing it and dominating.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1
